fault_hypothesis: Lines 72-73 incorrectly remove a duplicate element when both streams end with the same value. The condition `out[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]` triggers an erroneous `out.pop()` that removes one occurrence of a legitimately duplicated trailing element.

constraints.intent: After pop(), the output length should still equal the sum of both input lengths — this is violated when the pop incorrectly removes a duplicate.
After the then-branch executes the pop, the total count must still equal the combined input lengths.

spec.expr: not (out__1 and left and right and out__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(out__1) != len(left) + len(right)

constraints.intent: The pop condition should never be true when the output already has the correct total count, catching the erroneous removal.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
